title: Open the design system's sass channel on an Angular app
description: Wire ONE Angular app to the workspace's design system: add the sass load path to the app's build target (stylePreprocessorOptions.includePaths — the only in-repo channel, since the workspace links via tsconfig path aliases and SASS cannot read those), add the design system's styles to the build's `inputs` (CACHE CORRECTNESS: the app reaches the DS through a load path, not a TS import, so the Nx graph sees no edge and would otherwise replay a cached bundle carrying stale tokens), and upsert the `@use '<ds>/styles' as ds;` and `@include ds.theme();` marker blocks into the app's global stylesheet. Self-detecting (finds the design system by its `type:design-system` tag) and a clean no-op when the workspace has none. Composed by both the `design-system` generator (for apps that already exist) and the `app` generator (so a LATER app is wired with no flag). Idempotent and --repair-safe: arrays are merged and de-duplicated, and the stylesheet blocks are upserted between their markers.

project.x-prompt: Which application should consume the design system?
